context_of_use: Deterministic synthetic engineering evidence for ECG R-peak detection and observable beat-to-beat RR interval measurement, without a signal-quality claim.

acceptance_profile.description: Pre-specified detector and RR limits. Signal-quality classification and HRV are outside this claim.
Clean and rate-extreme cases must independently retain detector-grade event performance and 25 ms aggregate RR MAE.
The artifact case is gated separately so clean cases cannot hide detector or RR failure under baseline wander and powerline interference.

truth_policy.event_scoreability: R-peak truth requires a complete in-record QRS and more than 5% retained signal during all-lead dropout; every exclusion is explicitly reported.
truth_policy.rr_definition: Difference between consecutive present-QRS construction R-peak times inside the record; an interval touching unscoreable R-peak truth is not_evaluable.
truth_policy.first_beat: Excluded because no preceding in-record R peak exists for RR construction.
truth_policy.artifact_overlap: Baseline wander and powerline intervals remain scoreable and are separately visible in the artifact bin.
truth_policy.scope: R-peak event and RR measurement files are required. Signal-quality, HRV, classification and delineation outputs are explicitly outside the evidence matrix.
truth_policy.boundary: Only QRS truth whose constructed support crosses the record boundary is excluded; no blanket edge window is used.

evidence_boundary: Synthetic engineering evidence for R-peak detection and observable beat-to-beat RR measurement only; not clinical validation, diagnostic performance evidence, regulatory qualification, or evidence for signal quality or HRV.
